name: Vallabha-Balaka
dates_notes: Late-seventeenth- to mid-eighteenth-century Puṣṭimārga scholar; dated by manuscript and by the colophons of his commentaries on the Aṇu-Bhāṣya. The name 'Bālaka' is honorific within the lineage and not a marker of immaturity.
core_thesis:
Vallabha-Bālaka is the post-Puruṣottama scholastic of the *Puṣṭi-Mārga* whose principal work is sub-commentary on the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya*. The doctrinal architecture is preserved without alteration: *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* (the *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* on *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.1.14](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.1.14), on the *ārambhādhikaraṇa*); the rejection of *māyā-vāda* and *jagat-mithyātva* (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 1.1.4–5](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.4-5)); the *jīva*'s status as real *aṃśa* with concealed *ānanda* (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.3.43](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.3.43)); *Puṣṭi* as the operative soteriological principle (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 3.3–3.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.3-3.4) and 4.1–4.4, following Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation).

The contribution is technical-scholastic and is concentrated on the points where Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* and Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation are most compressed. Three loci do the load-bearing work. The *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* on the *ārambhādhikaraṇa* ([BS 2.1.14](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.1.14)) opens Vallabha's terse statement of *avikṛta-pariṇāma* against the alternative readings the school must reject (Śaṅkara's *vivartavāda*, Bhāskara's *aupādhika-pariṇāma*, Rāmānuja's *pariṇāma* qualified by *śarīra-śarīrin*, Madhva's strict *bheda*); the demarcation is made point by point in the form the lineage transmits in its higher *pāṭhaśālā* curriculum. The *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* on the *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa* ([BS 2.3.43](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.3.43)) fixes the *Puṣṭi-Mārga* reading of the *jīva*'s *aṃśa*-status against the rival readings, with particular care over the *ānanda*-concealment doctrine (*tirobhāva* of the *jīva*'s *ānanda* under *avidyā* identified as Kṛṣṇa's *śakti*, not as an independent ontological item). The *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* on the *adhikaraṇas* concerned with *sādhana* ([BS 3.3–3.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.3-3.4)) and the post-mortem state ([BS 4.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/4.4)) opens Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation, fixing the school's settled reading on the operation of *anugraha* and on participation in Kṛṣṇa's *līlā* through eternal *seva* as the form of *mokṣa*.

The work consolidates Puruṣottama's settled positions in a form locked to Vallabha's and Viṭṭhalanātha's *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* base-text. Together with Puruṣottama's *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa*, the *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* fixes the lineage's reading of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* in the form it is studied in modern Puṣṭimārga scholastic curricula.

engaged_works.title: Balaka-Bhashya on Anu-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Vallabha's and Viṭṭhalanātha's Aṇu-Bhāṣya.

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus in Puṣṭimārga textual scholarship.

engaged_works.summary: The *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* is Vallabha-Bālaka's sub-commentary on the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras* (Vallabha's portion through [BS 3.2.33](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.2.33) and Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation from [BS 3.2.34](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.2.34) through [BS 4.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/4.4)). The method is *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* exposition with the school's settled position fixed at each contested point. Four *adhikaraṇas* are particularly load-bearing. (i) The opening *jijñāsādhikaraṇa* (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 1.1.1](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.1)) reads the inquiry as inquiry into Brahman-as-Kṛṣṇa, against Śaṅkara's *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* construal that takes the inquiry as into a Brahman identified with the *jīva*-self by superimposition. (ii) The *māyā-adhikaraṇa* material (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 1.1.4–5](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.4-5)) follows Vallabha and Puruṣottama in identifying *māyā* with Kṛṣṇa's *śakti* (the *māyin* of *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on [BS 1.1.5](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.5) read consonantly with *śuddhādvaita*) rather than with epistemic *avidyā* or with an *anirvacanīya* third-thing. (iii) The *ārambhādhikaraṇa* (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.1.14](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.1.14)) opens Vallabha's compressed statement of *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* against the alternative readings the school must distinguish itself from — Śaṅkara's *vivarta*, Bhāskara's *aupādhika-pariṇāma*, Rāmānuja's *pariṇāma* qualified by *śarīra-śarīrin*, Madhva's strict *bheda*. (iv) The *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa* (*Bālaka-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.3.43](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.3.43)) fixes the school's reading of the *jīva*-Brahman relation as real *aṃśa*-relation with *ānanda*-concealment, against both identity-from-eternity and strict *bheda*. On the *sādhana*-adhikaraṇas ([BS 3.3–3.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.3-3.4)) and the *mokṣa*-adhikaraṇas ([BS 4.1–4.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/4.1-4.4)), the *Bālaka-Bhāṣya* opens Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation — particularly on the operation of *anugraha* ([BS 3.4.1–17](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.4.1-17)) and on the post-mortem state of the liberated as participation in Kṛṣṇa's *līlā* through eternal *seva* ([BS 4.4.1–22](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/4.4.1-22)). The work is the standard post-Puruṣottama Puṣṭimārga sub-commentary on the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* and is read in tandem with Puruṣottama's *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* in the school's higher curriculum.
